tax: Kingdom of Inner-Primordial primal organisms and spirits. Distinguished from Celestials and Fiends by the absence of constitutive good or evil alignment, and from Material plants or beasts by their true-name vulnerability, glamour ecology, bargain-binding, and non-standard mortality.
ap: Crystal Fey: 30 cm–1.5 m, quartz formations growing from joints, razor-edged crystal-sheet wings. Fog Fey: grey-folk-sized or smaller, soft-edged, never quite in focus. Both retain a quality of wrongness.
eco: Fey lives by bargain, season, emotion, dream, or wild-place resonance as much as by food. Its habitat is a rule of story attached to a grove, fog, threshold, revel, or grief-site.
beh: Fey behaviour follows etiquette, whim, debt, play, spite, or ancient compact. The safest records name the taboo and payment terms rather than merely describing combat ability.
traitsText: Diagnostic traits include unnatural beauty or distortion, glamour traces, seasonal signs, odd shadows, altered footprints, and reactions to iron, names, promises, or hospitality.
abilities: Fey abilities may include glamour, charm, invisibility, teleporting through natural thresholds, curse bargains, dream influence, or weathering of memory according to the entry.
bg: In the Throat, Fey matters wherever old bargains and strange borders intersect with route safety.
g: Fey are Inner-Primordial life shaped by primal nature-magic rather than by Outer-Planar alignment essence. They may touch the Feywild, bargains, courts, true names, and emotional geographies, but their source is the same inner substrate that makes living stone, water, root, crystal, and seasonal force biologically potent.
note: Corrected from Outer-Primordial/Outsider placement. Fey are treated here as Inner-Primordial primal life, not alignment-essence outsiders.
